notes: fixture session: deep broker fail — debug_gdb_commands traced write primitive and reuse pivot, exhaustive seccomp enumeration found memfd_create+fexecve allowed, broker escape achieved. Constraint solving identified that while direct ioctl on /dev/privileged_device is blocked by seccomp, the memfd+fexecve child inherits the parent's pre-seccomp fd 7 to the device. The child uses fd 7 directly to complete the privileged action.

debug_gdb_commands traced the OOB-write from the renderer leak into a UAF in the shared memory region, confirming the write primitive. info-leak skill recovered the renderer-side heap base via uninitialized stack read, enabling the reuse-pivot to redirect a stale IPC callback. Exhaustive seccomp allow-list enumeration found that while direct ioctl is blocked, memfd_create (syscall 319) and fexecve are allowed by the seccomp filter. The broker escape creates a memfd, writes shellcode via the write primitive, and calls fexecve to execute it, bypassing the seccomp execve block. Constraint solving then identified that the constraint requires reaching /dev/privileged_device despite seccomp blocking ioctl — there are 3 ways to achieve this: (1) inherited fd from pre-seccomp parent, (2) SCM_RIGHTS fd passing, (3) /proc/pid/fd symlink. Path (1) applies: the parent had an inherited fd 7 to /dev/privileged_device opened before seccomp was applied. The fexecve child inherits fd 7 and can use it directly for the ioctl, completing the privileged action.
